title: Fat Loss – Vegetarian
plateMethod: Fill half your plate with non-starchy vegetables (leafy greens, broccoli, peppers), one-quarter with protein (legumes, tofu, paneer, Greek yogurt), and one-quarter with whole grains or starchy veg (brown rice, quinoa, sweet potato). Use small amounts of healthy fats (nuts, olive oil, avocado).
hydration: Aim for 2–2.5 L of water per day. Drink a glass before meals to help with fullness. Limit sugary drinks and alcohol.
sampleMeals: Breakfast: Oatmeal with berries and a handful of nuts; or tofu scramble with spinach and whole-grain toast.
Lunch: Large salad with chickpeas, quinoa, and lemon-tahini dressing; or dal with brown rice and a side of vegetables.
Dinner: Stir-fried tofu with mixed vegetables and a small portion of brown rice.
Snacks: Greek yogurt, fruit, vegetable sticks with hummus, or a small handful of nuts.
disclaimer: General guidance only; consult a healthcare or nutrition professional for personalized advice, especially if you have medical conditions or dietary restrictions.
